questions.context:
The table shows the distances (km) cycled by 60 members of a cycling club in one month.

| Distance (km) | Frequency |
|---------------|-----------|
| 0 < d ≤ 50   | 8         |
| 50 < d ≤ 100 | 18        |
| 100 < d ≤ 150| 22        |
| 150 < d ≤ 200| 9         |
| 200 < d ≤ 300| 3         |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out an estimate for the mean distance cycled.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Show that the class containing the median is 100 < d ≤ 150.

parts.label: d
parts.questionText: Give a reason why the mean is more useful than the median for this data.
parts.correctAnswer: The mean uses all values in the calculation, so it gives a more complete summary of the data

markScheme.desc: Mean uses all data values / gives a more representative average when no extreme outliers oe

questions.context:
A cumulative frequency table is given for the heights (cm) of 100 plants.

| Height (cm)  | Cumulative frequency |
|--------------|----------------------|
| ≤ 20         | 10                   |
| ≤ 30         | 28                   |
| ≤ 40         | 60                   |
| ≤ 50         | 85                   |
| ≤ 60         | 100                  |

parts.label: b

markScheme.desc: Q1 at cf=25, Q3 at cf=75
Q1 ≈ 29 cm, Q3 ≈ 46 cm (from interpolation, accept ±2)

workingSteps: Q1 at cf = 25: between cf=10 (h=20) and cf=28 (h=30)
Q3 at cf = 75: between cf=60 (h=40) and cf=85 (h=50)

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Estimate the number of plants with height greater than 45 cm.

markScheme.desc: Reads cf at h=45 from graph ≈ 77; then 100−77

workingSteps: At h=45: between cf=60 (h=40) and cf=85 (h=50)

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
Two classes each have 25 students.
Class A: mean = 68, standard deviation = 5.
Class B: mean = 68, standard deviation = 12.

Describe two differences between the test performance of the two classes.
parts.correctAnswer: Same average performance; Class B has much more variable/spread-out results

markScheme.desc: Both classes have the same mean/average score oe
Class B is more spread out / less consistent / higher standard deviation oe

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
The two classes are combined into one group of 50 students.
Work out the mean score for the combined group.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Give a reason why the combined standard deviation would be greater than 5 but less than 12.
parts.correctAnswer: The combined group includes values from both distributions; the spread is somewhere between the two individual spreads

markScheme.desc: The combined data includes both the tightly spread Class A and widely spread Class B values, so the overall spread is between the two oe
